findings.evidence: `Characteristic.lean:64` already defines `characteristicVectors`, but `Weight.lean:86` uses `def chi (k : V → ℤ) ... : ℤ`; `two_mul_chi` at `Weight.lean:96` needs `hk`, showing the defining doubled formula only holds on the characteristic subdomain.
findings.fix: Make `chi` take `k : P.characteristicVectors` or an explicit `hk : P.IsCharacteristicVector k`, or else define the unrestricted expression in `ℚ` and provide the integer-valued characteristic specialization.
findings.issue: `chi` is exposed on every covector `k : V → ℤ`, so for non-characteristic `k` it silently returns an integer division value even when `-(k(x)+x·x)/2` is not an integer; the intended `χ_k` is defined only for characteristic covectors.

summary: The new parity lemmas match the existing intersection-form convention, but the exposed `chi` definition has the wrong domain for the claimed integer-valued lattice-homology function.
